description:
You are helping a small web-dev team automate their very simple “asset build” step.
    Everything you need to touch lives inside the project root
    /home/user/web_project

    Initial project layout (already on disk):
        /home/user/web_project/src/css/style1.css
        /home/user/web_project/src/css/style2.css
        /home/user/web_project/src/js/app.js
        /home/user/web_project/src/js/utils.js
        /home/user/web_project/src/index.html
        /home/user/web_project/src/about.html

    Goal: write a POSIX-shell script named
        /home/user/web_project/build_assets.sh
    that performs the following actions any time it is run.

    1. Recreate a clean output directory
           /home/user/web_project/dist
       • If that directory already exists, remove it first (only that dir,
         not the whole project) and then recreate it.

    2. Concatenate assets
       • Collect every “*.css” file anywhere under src/ (there are only two
         right now, but do NOT hard-code their names).  The list **must** be
         the result of  
             find src -type f -name '*.css' | sort
         Concatenate them in exactly that order into
             /home/user/web_project/dist/style.bundle.css
       • Do the same for “*.js” files, writing the result to
             /home/user/web_project/dist/app.bundle.js

    3. Generate a SHA-256 manifest  
       • For every source file with extension css, js **or** html
         (total of six files at the moment) compute its SHA-256 checksum.  
       • Write a file   
             /home/user/web_project/dist/manifest.txt  
         where each line is formatted exactly like a normal `sha256sum`
         output:  
             <64-char-hex-checksum><two-spaces><relative_path_from_project_root>
         Lines **must** be sorted alphabetically by the relative path.
         Example line layout (checksum shown here is made-up):
             e3b0c44298fc1c14...  src/css/style1.css

    4. Produce an HTML statistics log  
       • Using `wc -l` (or equivalent), count the number of lines in every
         “*.html” file under src/.  
       • Create  
             /home/user/web_project/dist/html_stats.log  
         with one line per file, sorted alphabetically by the relative path,
         in the exact layout:  
             <relative_path> <line_count>
         Example:
             src/about.html 1

    5. Record a one-line build summary  
       • After everything above succeeds, create  
             /home/user/web_project/dist/build.log  
         containing a single line made of:
             <UTC_ISO-8601_timestamp>Z <total_source_file_count>
         Where <total_source_file_count> is the number of files whose
         checksums you wrote to manifest.txt (should be 6 with the current
         tree).  Example pattern (your timestamp will differ):
             2027-12-04T18:25:11Z 6

    6. The very last action of the script must be to `echo "Build complete"` to
       STDOUT so CI knows the script ran to the end.

    7. Make the script itself executable.

    IMPORTANT FORMAT RULES THE TESTER WILL CHECK
    ------------------------------------------------
      • dist/style.bundle.css        – concatenation order exactly matches
        the lexicographically sorted `find` output.
      • dist/app.bundle.js           – same rule for JS.
      • dist/manifest.txt            – each line must match the canonical
        `sha256sum` output for that file, double-space separator, sorted
        alphabetically by path.
      • dist/html_stats.log          – lines sorted alphabetically, one space
        separator, NO extra characters.
      • dist/build.log               – exactly one line, ISO-8601 UTC stamp
        ending in “Z”, one space, integer 6.
      • The script must emit the text “Build complete” (without quotes,
        with a trailing newline) to STDOUT.

    You do NOT need sudo.  Everything should work with 5–10 normal POSIX
    shell commands (find, sort, cat, sha256sum, wc, mkdir, rm, echo, date,
    etc.).  Feel free to inspect the existing files before scripting.

    When you are done, the following files must exist and be non-empty:
        /home/user/web_project/build_assets.sh  (executable)
        /home/user/web_project/dist/style.bundle.css
        /home/user/web_project/dist/app.bundle.js
        /home/user/web_project/dist/manifest.txt
        /home/user/web_project/dist/html_stats.log
        /home/user/web_project/dist/build.log
truth:
1. Pre-existing source files and their exact contents (each file ends with a single newline): /home/user/web_project/src/css/style1.css body { background: #fff; }\n /home/user/web_project/src/css/style2.css h1 { color: #333; }\n /home/user/web_project/src/js/app.js console.log('App loaded');\n /home/user/web_project/src/js/utils.js function sum(a,b){return a+b;}\n /home/user/web_project/src/index.html Home

Home

\n /home/user/web_project/src/about.html About

About

\n 2. When the agent’s script runs correctly: • /home/user/web_project/dist will be newly created and contain the five artefacts named in the public task description. • dist/style.bundle.css will be exactly the concatenation of style1.css followed by style2.css (because of sort order). • dist/app.bundle.js will be the concatenation of app.js then utils.js. • dist/manifest.txt will contain six lines. The harness will compute `sha256sum` on each source file and assert that every line in manifest.txt matches the pattern ^[0-9a-f]{64} src/.*$ and that the checksum itself is correct. • dist/html_stats.log will contain exactly: src/about.html 1\n src/index.html 1\n • dist/build.log will match the regex ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z 6\n$ • The script will emit the phrase “Build complete” (with newline). 3. Permissions: • The harness will `chmod +x` build_assets.sh when verifying, but the agent itself must also set the executable bit (to demonstrate it knows how). 4. The automated test workflow: a. Check that build_assets.sh exists and is executable. b. Run it from inside /home/user/web_project. c. Verify STDOUT ends with “Build complete\n”. d. Perform the file/content checks listed above. No other directories or files will be checked.
